title: Vowels Part 1: Short Sounds
description: Hindi vowels (स्वर) are the foundation of every word. Unlike English, Hindi makes a clear distinction between SHORT and LONG vowels — and the length changes the meaning of words! In this episode, we'll learn the four short vowels: अ (a), इ (i), उ (u), and ऋ (ri). These are quick, clipped sounds — don't hold them too long!
keyPoints: Hindi has 13 vowels (स्वर), divided into short (ह्रस्व) and long (दीर्घ)
Short vowels are quick and clipped — don't stretch them
The 4 short vowels: अ (a), इ (i), उ (u), ऋ (ri)
Vowel length changes word meanings in Hindi

vietnamese: a — nguyên âm ngắn 'a' như trong 'abrupt'

hint: Start with the vertical line on the left, then add the curved shape, and finish with the headline (शिरोरेखा) on top.

vietnamese: i — nguyên âm ngắn 'i' như trong 'bit'

hint: Draw the curved body first, then add the headline (शिरोरेखा) on top and the dot above.

prompt: Which vowel makes the sound like 'i' in 'bit'?
text: 'bit' के 'i' जैसी ध्वनि कौन सा स्वर बनाता है?
subtext: Think of a short, clipped 'i' sound

vietnamese: u — nguyên âm ngắn 'u' như trong 'put'

hint: Start with the vertical stroke, then add the curved tail at the bottom. Don't forget the headline (शिरोरेखा).

english: ri — as in 'trick' (a special vowel, rare but important)
vietnamese: ri — như trong 'trick' (nguyên âm đặc biệt, hiếm nhưng quan trọng)

hint: This is a complex letter. Start with the vertical stroke, add the curved hook, and finish with the headline.

prompt: Which word starts with the short vowel उ?
text: कौन सा शब्द ह्रस्व स्वर उ से शुरू होता है?
subtext: Look for the 'u' as in 'put' sound

prompt: Which letter represents the short 'a' sound?
text: ह्रस्व 'अ' ध्वनि का अक्षर कौन सा है?
subtext: The most common vowel in Hindi!

title: Short Vowels — Key Takeaways
takeaways: अ (a) — short 'a' as in 'abrupt', the most common sound in Hindi
इ (i) — short 'i' as in 'bit', quick and clipped
उ (u) — short 'u' as in 'put', rounded lips
ऋ (ri) — special vowel, sounds like 'ri' in 'trick'
Short vowels are quick — don't stretch them into long vowels!
Example words: अमर (immortal), इमली (tamarind), उल्लू (owl), ऋषि (sage)
